message:
fj-trace: validate scatter indices, updates dtype, and updates shape

Extend infer_scatter in crates/fj-trace/src/lib.rs so scatter primitive
shape inference rejects malformed operand/indices/updates triples at
trace time, instead of deferring the check to codegen or silently
accepting a nonsensical HLO body.

Validation added to infer_scatter:
- Indices dtype must be integral. I32/I64/U32/U64/Bool are accepted;
  any floating-point or complex dtype now fails with a shape inference
  error whose detail reads "scatter indices must be integral dtype,
  got <dtype>".
- Updates dtype must match the operand dtype exactly; a mismatch fails
  with a detail string naming both dtypes so the diagnostic points at
  the offending input pair.
- Updates shape must equal <indices.shape ++ operand.shape[1..]>. This
  is the standard XLA scatter contract for single-axis scatter: the
  updates array carries one slice per index, and each slice has the
  shape of the trailing dims of the operand. The check rebuilds the
  expected dim vector and compares it against updates.shape.dims.

Three new tests exercise each failure path using run_logged_test so the
evidence ledger records the failure:
- scatter_rejects_updates_shape_mismatch — [3,2] operand, 2-vector
  indices, 4-vector updates; asserts the error text mentions
  "updates shape".
- scatter_rejects_updates_dtype_mismatch — F64 operand + I64 updates;
  asserts the error text mentions "updates dtype".
- scatter_rejects_non_integral_indices — F64 indices; asserts the
  error text mentions "indices must be integral".

Each test is fingerprinted with a fixture_id derived from a stable
tuple so the artifacts are reproducible across runs.
